Build logs, security deep-dives, and maker notes from a solo developer shipping privacy-first software. No fluff. Honest progress reports. Real engineering decisions explained.

Security Published
Argon2id vs PBKDF2: Why We Upgraded LockedPages
PBKDF2 at 600,000 iterations takes 150 ms on a modern CPU — and 2 ms on a GPU. Argon2id is the OWASP #1 recommendation for a reason. Here is the full migration story.
Deep Dive
2026-06-188 min read
→
Privacy Draft
Zero-Knowledge by Design: Architecture Notes for miLife
How to build an app where even the developer cannot read user data. Encryption key derivation, local-first storage, and what "zero-knowledge" actually means in practice.
Deep Dive
In progress~10 min read
⋯
GameDev Planned
Building IRIS: An AI Director for Match-3
Series: Galaxy Diamond Devlog · Part 1
Galaxy Diamond's AI Director adapts difficulty in real-time across 4 states. This is how the state machine works, what metrics it reads, and why a naive difficulty curve fails players.
Engineering
Planned~12 min read
⋯
Tools Draft
Clip Trimmer: From 0 to 55 Tests in One Month
The testing philosophy behind a media automation pipeline: pytest, ruff, CI/CD, and why testing video output is fundamentally different from testing business logic.
Build Log
In progress~6 min read
⋯
Research Planned
Content-Addressed Archives: Why .apx Knows What's Inside
Zip treats files as a flat list. The .apx format treats archives as a content graph. This is the design philosophy, the rolling hash approach, and why deduplication needs to be first-class.
Research
Planned~14 min read
⋯
GameDev Planned
Unity 6 ScriptableObjects: The Architecture Behind Mini Motor Maze
Series: Mini Motor Maze Devlog · Part 1
ScriptableObjects as the single source of truth for car stats, track configs, and level data. The pattern that lets us balance 20 levels without touching a line of code.
Engineering
